name: Redcap
description: Redcaps are malevolent goblin-like creatures from the Scottish Borders folklore. They are said to inhabit ruined castles, particularly those associated with bloodshed and violence. Redcaps are known for their murderous nature, dipping their caps in the blood of their victims to keep them stained a vivid red. They are incredibly strong and agile, wielding a formidable pike or other weapon. Redcaps are believed to be immortal unless their cap fades, at which point they will die. They are often associated with specific locations and are bound to those places, unable to leave without losing their strength. These creatures are considered dangerous and unpredictable, posing a significant threat to travelers who stray too close to their lairs. They are also said to enjoy reciting riddles and rhymes, often containing threats or warnings. Redcaps represent the darker, more violent aspects of Scottish folklore and serve as a cautionary tale against trespassing or disrespecting the spirits of the land. Their existence highlights the belief in territorial spirits and the importance of respecting boundaries, both physical and supernatural. They are often depicted as squat, muscular figures with long, sharp teeth and glowing red eyes, embodying the brutal history of the Scottish Borders.
subtitle: Blood-soaked goblin of ruined castles
appearance: Redcaps are typically described as short, stocky, and muscular creatures with long, grey hair and sharp teeth. Their eyes glow red, and they always wear a cap soaked in human blood. Their skin is often depicted as being tough and leathery, and they are usually armed with a pike or other weapon.
habitat: Ruined castles, particularly those with a history of violence and bloodshed, located in the Scottish Borders.
behavior: Redcaps are fiercely territorial and aggressive, attacking anyone who trespasses on their domain. They are known for their brutal and merciless killings, often leaving their victims' bodies as a warning to others. They are also said to enjoy reciting riddles and rhymes, often containing threats or warnings. They are bound to their lairs and cannot leave without losing their strength.

keyMyths.title: The Redcap of Hermitage Castle
keyMyths.description: Hermitage Castle is said to be haunted by a Redcap who guards its ruins, attacking anyone who dares to disturb its peace. The Redcap is said to have been created from the violent history of the castle.

sources.author: Walter Scott
sources.description: A collection of ballads and folklore from the Scottish Borders, featuring descriptions of Redcaps.
